summary: Well-executed blueprint-state file that reports concrete, evidence-backed implementation status for Dashboard Terminal V1 against its ideal and implementation-resolved companions. Each CurrentReality concept carries specific verifiable claims (packages exist, gateway routes present, mobile screenshots taken, shell noise documented). The file correctly positions itself as a comparison document rather than a standalone spec, explicitly names the three companion blueprints it compares against, and satisfies the development-process requirement for blueprint-state tracking. Moderate obsolescence risk because the content is snapshot-in-time (specific screenshot paths, specific V1 status) with no internal mechanism to signal staleness if the terminal feature evolves.
strengths: Every CurrentReality concept has a concrete .means() block with verifiable factual claims rather than vague status labels
Explicitly names all three companion blueprints it compares against in the defines block, making layer dependence transparent
Includes a specific screenshot path as durable verification evidence, grounding the assessment in observable artifact
The when/then blocks correctly map existence of evidence to assessment recording, maintaining clean causal structure
Stays disciplined at the blueprint-state layer — reports what is implemented and what remains as polish, never drifts into redesigning the ideal
Directly fulfills the BlueprintStateTracksCurrentReality rule from development-process.agentish.ts with status, confidence, evidence, gaps, and known issues
weaknesses: Contains a line comment ('// Dashboard Terminal Blueprint State') which the language root explicitly rejects
Mobile verification claims overlap between mobileVerified and mobileReadableOutput — the distinction is valid but the boundary could be sharper to reduce near-duplicate coverage

No internal staleness signal or timestamp convention — if the terminal feature gains new capabilities or fixes the shell-noise issue, this file will silently drift without any structural indicator that it needs update
The workflowAlignment concept is more of a process-compliance assertion than an implementation-reality claim, making it slightly out of character for the other CurrentReality entries
